items.prompt: Decide whether the table `(2, 6), (4, 12), (7, 21)` is proportional and justify the constant of proportionality.
items.expected_evidence: explain statements such as 2 to 3 or 3 for every 5 as coordinated multiplicative relationships
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates explain statements such as 2 to 3 or 3 for every 5 as coordinated multiplicative relationships with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Find the unit rate for 18 miles in 3 hours and explain what it means.
items.expected_evidence: match verbal, pictorial, tabular, and symbolic ratio representations in bounded cases
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates match verbal, pictorial, tabular, and symbolic ratio representations in bounded cases with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Find 15% of 80 and explain how the percent is acting as a rate per hundred.
items.expected_evidence: reject additive interpretations when a task is ratio-based
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates reject additive interpretations when a task is ratio-based with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Explain why equal differences alone do not prove a relationship is proportional.
items.expected_evidence: explain statements such as 2 to 3 or 3 for every 5 as coordinated multiplicative relationships
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains the structure coherently and ties the explanation back to explain statements such as 2 to 3 or 3 for every 5 as coordinated multiplicative relationships.

items.prompt: Explain how a unit rate and a constant of proportionality describe the same multiplicative structure.
items.expected_evidence: match verbal, pictorial, tabular, and symbolic ratio representations in bounded cases
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains the structure coherently and ties the explanation back to match verbal, pictorial, tabular, and symbolic ratio representations in bounded cases.

items.prompt: A student says a table is proportional because it increases by 3 each row. Diagnose the additive-pattern error. Focus on additive comparison substitution.
items.expected_evidence: explain statements such as 2 to 3 or 3 for every 5 as coordinated multiplicative relationships
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies the dominant breakdown, names the structural issue accurately, and redirects the work toward explain statements such as 2 to 3 or 3 for every 5 as coordinated multiplicative relationships.

items.prompt: A student scales one quantity by 2 but the other by 3 and still claims proportionality. Identify the breakdown. Focus on part part part whole collapse.
items.expected_evidence: match verbal, pictorial, tabular, and symbolic ratio representations in bounded cases
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies the dominant breakdown, names the structural issue accurately, and redirects the work toward match verbal, pictorial, tabular, and symbolic ratio representations in bounded cases.

items.prompt: A map uses 1 cm for every 5 km. How far does 7.2 cm represent, and why is the relationship proportional?
items.expected_evidence: explain statements such as 2 to 3 or 3 for every 5 as coordinated multiplicative relationships
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves explain statements such as 2 to 3 or 3 for every 5 as coordinated multiplicative relationships in a changed surface form or representation.

items.prompt: A recipe uses 3 cups of flour for 2 batches. Write an equation relating flour to batches and find the amount for 5 batches.
items.expected_evidence: match verbal, pictorial, tabular, and symbolic ratio representations in bounded cases
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves match verbal, pictorial, tabular, and symbolic ratio representations in bounded cases in a changed surface form or representation.

items.prompt: On a delayed check, determine whether 4 notebooks for $6 and 10 notebooks for $15 are proportional and justify your reasoning.
items.expected_evidence: explain statements such as 2 to 3 or 3 for every 5 as coordinated multiplicative relationships
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner still demonstrates explain statements such as 2 to 3 or 3 for every 5 as coordinated multiplicative relationships on a delayed recheck without a blocking recurrence.

items.prompt: Probe fraction magnitude beyond one whole before diagnosing ratio as multiplicative comparison, and state whether the learner is missing multiplicative structure or only the current proportional application.
items.expected_evidence: shows whether fraction magnitude beyond one whole is secure enough to support ratio as multiplicative comparison
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner response makes it possible to distinguish an upstream gap in fraction magnitude beyond one whole from a current-node issue in ratio as multiplicative comparison.

items.prompt: Probe multiplicative comparison and scaling before diagnosing ratio as multiplicative comparison, and state whether the learner is missing multiplicative structure or only the current proportional application.
items.expected_evidence: shows whether multiplicative comparison and scaling is secure enough to support ratio as multiplicative comparison
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner response makes it possible to distinguish an upstream gap in multiplicative comparison and scaling from a current-node issue in ratio as multiplicative comparison.
